name: Candidate C1
answers: I improved API reliability by adding retries, circuit breakers, and clear SLO monitoring with weekly incident review.
The reason this helped is because we reduced cascading failures and made deployment risk visible to the team.

name: Candidate C4
answers: I built a churn model with calibrated probabilities and ran A/B targeting that improved retention by 6.4%.
Because model drift was likely, I added monthly retraining triggers and feature monitoring.

name: Candidate C5
answers: I migrated a monolith checkout path to event-driven services, cutting rollback frequency and reducing timeout spikes.
Therefore we introduced idempotency keys and partitioned queues to isolate noisy tenants.

name: Candidate C6
answers: I tuned warehouse queries by rewriting joins and indexing fact tables, lowering report refresh from 18 minutes to 4.
For instance, we validated query plans weekly and documented ownership so regressions were caught early.
